DEFENDANT FCA US LLC'S MOTION TO TAX COSTS.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Motion For Attorney Fees, Costs, And Expenses
Off Calendar Sep-05-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. GREGORY SOGOYAN, ESQ. of Strategic Legal Practices (Tel: 310-929-4900) appearing via CourtCall for the plaintiff;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF STEPHANIE NEHMENS' Motion For Attorney Fees, Costs, And Expenses.

Argument presented by plaintiff's counsel. Matter submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Stephanie Nehmens's motion for attorney fees, costs, and expenses is granted in the amount of $50,817.92 in attorney's fees and $7,051.23 in costs and expenses. In defendant's December 11, 2018 Code of Civil Procedure section 998 offer, which plaintiff accepted on January 15, 2019, defendant offered "to pay reasonable costs, expenses and attorney's fees based on actual time expended pursuant to Civil Code section 1794(b) as stipulated by the parties or, if the parties cannot agree, upon motion to the Court having jurisdiction over this action." (Cutler Decl., Ex. 4.) Despite the emphasized language, plaintiff did not make any effort to stipulate to an award of reasonable attorneys' fees, costs and expenses until August 12, 2019, after counsel had already expended nearly $9,000 in attorney's fees in preparing the instant motion. (Id. 24; Shahian Decl., Ex. 31.) Further, a large number of attorneys (nine) billed time on the case on plaintiff's behalf, which necessarily resulted in substantial duplication and inefficiency. For these reasons, the Court finds that not all of the time for which plaintiff seeks compensation was necessary and reasonably incurred. (See Etcheson v. FCA US LLC (2018) 30 Cal.App.5th 831, 840 ["A prevailing buyer has the burden of 'showing that the fees incurred were "allowable," were "reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation," and were "reasonable in amount."'"].) In order to determine a reasonable award, the Court declines to award any compensation for time spent in connection with the fee motion, which could have been entirely avoided, and has reduced the remaining time by 25%, to $50,817.92, which the Court finds to be a reasonable lodestar. (See, e.g., Warren v. Kia Motors America, Inc. (2018) 30 Cal.App.5th 24, 37, 40-41 [trial court may apply a negative multiplier to adjust the lodestar figure to arrive at a reasonable fee if it "clearly explain[s] its case-specific reasons for the percentage reduction," which may properly include excessive time spent on case by multiple attorneys]; Save Our Uniquely Rural Community Environment v. County of San Bernardino (2015) 235 Cal.App.4th 1179, 1188 [trial court did not abuse its discretion in reducing lodestar by 80 percent].) The Court declines to award a positive multiplier, as plaintiff has not shown that this "lemon law" case presented novel or difficult questions of law or fact. (See Hall v. FCA US LLC (E.D. Cal. May 21, 2018) 2018 WL 2298431 at *8 [declining to award multiplier to plaintiff's counsel in Song-Beverly Act case where, among other things, case did not present novel or difficult questions of law or fact, there was no evidence that the nature of the litigation precluded other employment by the attorneys, and the contingent nature of the fee award was outweighed by the other factors].)

At plaintiff's counsel's request, the Court authorizes an addition to its ruling that defendant pay plaintiff within 30 days of the date of the court's formal order.

Plaintiff's counsel is to prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano, CSR#8600, e-mail: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
